title: 3D tilt card with inertia on release
titleAr: بطاقة 3D مائلة مع قصور ذاتي عند التحرير
desc: Track rotation velocity each frame, then on pointerup let the card keep spinning briefly with exponential decay instead of snapping flat.
descAr: تتبع سرعة الدوران كل إطار، وعند رفع المؤشر دع البطاقة تستمر في الدوران للحظة مع توهين أسي بدلاً من العودة الفورية.
summary: Most 3D tilt cards snap back to flat instantly on release, losing the tactile feel. Adding inertia with exponential decay makes the interaction feel physical and polished.
problem:
Most 3D tilt implementations snap back to flat immediately on pointer release. This feels robotic and misses a chance for tactile, physical feedback. Users expect inertia from a physical 3D surface — the sudden stop breaks the illusion.

The problem is structural: developers treat tilt as a hover-only effect and never consider what happens after the cursor leaves. A card that tracks rotation but has no momentum feels weightless.
problemTitle: Cards snap flat instantly on release
solution: On pointermove, compute delta-rotation per frame and store it in a velocity vector. On pointerup, continue applying that velocity each frame multiplied by a friction coefficient (e.g. 0.95). Stop when velocity drops below a threshold. The card coasts to rest naturally. Tap it gently and it barely moves; flick it hard and it spins dramatically before settling.
solutionTitle: Track velocity, then apply exponential decay

callout.text: Key idea: friction should be subtle (0.92–0.97 per frame). Too low and the card stops instantly; too high and it never settles. Test with real interaction to find the sweet spot.

before.desc: On pointerup, rotation snaps instantly to 0,0,0.
before.code: // Resets immediately — no feel el.addEventListener('pointerup', () => { el.style.transform = 'rotateX(0) rotateY(0)'; // transitions back in 200ms });

after.desc: Card coasts to rest with exponential decay.
after.code: // Each frame after release velX *= 0.95; // friction velY *= 0.95; angleX += velX; angleY += velY; // Stop when velocity is negligible if (Math.abs(velX) < 0.01 && Math.abs(velY) < 0.01) { stopRAFLoop(); }
